summary: Introduces the imperfect -ía ending for -er/-ir verbs, contrasts it with the conditional (-ería vs -ía), and teaches several uses of reflexive 'se' including 'se vende' (for sale) and 'cómo se dice' (how do you say). Also covers quedar (to remain/be left) vs quedarse (to stay).

teachings.summary: For -er/-ir verbs in the imperfect, remove the ending and add -ía. This is the same sound as the conditional, but the conditional adds onto the full verb while the imperfect removes the ending first.
teachings.rule: Remove -er/-ir, add -ía (I/he/she/it/usted), -ías (tú), -íamos (we), -ían (they). Key contrast: comería (I would eat) vs comía (I was eating).

examples.english: I was eating / He was eating

teachings.notes: Conditional adds -ía to full verb (comería). Imperfect removes ending first (comía). Same -ía sound, different base.

teachings.summary: Se vende literally means 'it sells itself' and is used to mean 'for sale'. This is seen on houses and cars in Spanish-speaking countries.
teachings.notes: You will see 'Se vende' on houses and cars in Spanish-speaking places.

teachings.summary: How do you say? in Spanish is literally 'how does it say itself?' - another creative use of the reflexive se structure.

examples.english: How do you say? (How does it say itself?)

teachings.notes: Don't say '¿Cómo dices?' for this meaning - that means 'how do you (individually) say it?'

teachings.summary: Introduced present tense conjugation of decir. Irregular in he/she form (dice) but the we form escapes the irregularity.
teachings.rule: dice (he/she/it), dices (tú), dicen (they), decimos (we - regular pattern from decir)
teachings.notes: The we form tends to escape irregularity as the accent falls somewhere else.

teachings.summary: Quedar means to remain/be left over. Quedarse (reflexive) means to stay. 'Me queda uno' = I have one left (it remains to me one). 'Me quedo' = I stay (I remain myself).
teachings.rule: Quedar + indirect object = to have left (me queda uno). Quedarse (reflexive) = to stay (me quedo).
teachings.notes: The verb changes to plural when what remains is plural: me queda uno, me quedan dos.

teachings.summary: Se habla español means 'Spanish is spoken' or 'Spanish spoken' - literally 'Spanish speaks itself'. Another creative application of the reflexive se.
